{"key_event":"Federation and Copperbelt nationalist mobilization","reasoning":"Copper boom revenues, federal institutions, and coercive bans/detentions increased colonial-state capacity, but legitimacy was increasingly contested and administration remained bounded by African resistance. Civil society rose markedly as urban workers, mine unions, churches, newspapers, and mass nationalist parties—despite repression and fragmentation—created sustained African mobilization against federation and colonial rule.","society_change":1.2,"society_power":6.5,"state_change":0.7,"state_power":7.5}
